agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver 2025 Homeless Count results presented to Council
agenda_briefs.summary: Council received the 2025 Homeless Count report showing 2,715 people identified as homeless in Vancouver on March 11, 2025 (1,952 sheltered, 763 unsheltered), a 12% increase from 2023. 79% of respondents reported being homeless for at least a year. Indigenous peoples are 18 times more likely to be homeless than non-Indigenous peoples, making up 42% of respondents. Black and African diaspora peoples make up 9% of respondents yet only 1% of Vancouver's population. 83% of respondents indicated they are on income assistance or PWD. 42% of respondents reported their housing loss related to an eviction.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.	2025 Homeless Count – Vancouver
(no speakers per section 7.2(d) of the Procedure By-law)
Report
Presentation
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2026 Cultural Grants for Operating & Spaces
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on proposed 2026 Cultural Grants totaling $5,509,770 to 85 organizations. Operating grants include $2,828,160 for 5 institutions, $709,760 for 26 annual organizations, and $1,897,550/year for 3 years (2026-2028) for 52 multi-year organizations. Also proposed: a $75,000 grant to Vancouver Concert Hall and Theatre Society for Phase II feasibility study site assessment, and a $728,000 one-time grant to Historic Joy Kogawa House Society for hazardous materials abatement and accessibility upgrades. Some recommendations require a two-thirds majority vote under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed now — watch for the council vote on April 14, 2026 to see if your organization may be eligible for future funding.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
2.	2026 Cultural Grants (Balance of Operating, Cultural Spaces)
Recommendations A to C, E and G require at least 2/3 affirmative votes of all Council members under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver Community Sport Event Grants for Spring 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on awarding 19 grants totalling $89,000 to community sport events in Vancouver during Spring 2026. The grants range from $1,000 to $5,000 per event, with events scheduled between May 1 and August 30, 2026. The program budget is $244,000 for 2026, with a second intake in Fall 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you run a community sport event in Vancouver, watch for the council decision on April 14, 2026 — this could mean grant funding for your Spring 2026 event.

key_dates.label: Council vote on Spring 2026 sport event grants

agenda_briefs.source_body:
3.	Vancouver Community Sport Event Grants – Spring 2026 Intake
Recommendation A requires at least 2/3 affirmative votes of all Council members under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby property tax distribution for 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is being asked to approve a 2026 general purpose tax levy of $1,224,250,000 with no tax shift. The proposed distribution keeps the residential share at approximately 57.5% and the non-residential share at approximately 42.5%, based on adjustments and non-market changes to the assessment roll. Tax rates for Classes 5 (Light industry) and 6 (Business/other) will continue to be calculated on a blended basis, and Classes 1, 8, and 9 will share a common rate.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 14, 2026 — it will determine your 2026 property tax rates.

key_dates.label: Council vote on 2026 property tax distribution

agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Construction & Development
agenda_briefs.source_body:
4.	2026 Property Taxation: Distribution of Property Tax Levy
4a. Budget Resolution
4b. Report

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2025 property tax exemptions for non-profits
agenda_briefs.summary: Council received the 2025 Property Tax Exemptions report. In 2025, $60.9 billion of properties (12% of the Assessment Roll) were exempt, resulting in $335.1 million in forgone general purpose taxes, partially offset by $34.8 million in compensating payments-in-lieu of taxes (PILTs). Any net forgone taxes are redistributed to other taxpayers. Statutory exemptions apply to Crown lands, City property, charitable institutions, learning institutions, hospitals, churches, emergency shelters, and others. Permissive exemptions are discretionary by Council by-law.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
5.	2025 Property Tax Exemptions
(no speakers per section 7.2(d) of the Procedure By-law)
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Coastal Flood Management Policy
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on adopting a citywide Coastal Flood Management Policy. The policy clarifies the City's role in coastal flood management and identifies when the City may build new flood infrastructure. It emphasizes leveraging land-use authority and redevelopment to improve long-term flood resilience. City-built structural measures outside redevelopment will be considered only in specific situations, focused on preserving City facilities and core services, subject to financial capacity. The policy formalizes existing practices for consistency.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Check if your property is in a proposed flood-risk zone and attend the public hearing to have your say.

key_dates.label: Coastal Flood Management Policy public hearing

agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance
agenda_briefs.source_body:
6.	Coastal Flood Management Policy

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on Vancouver Heritage Foundation Board annual report
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on approving the Vancouver Heritage Foundation's 2025 Annual Report and a $152,375 operating grant for 2026, the first year of a three-year agreement (2026-2028). The grant funds staffing and resources for educational programming, activities, and conservation work. Approval requires at least two-thirds of all council members under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote on April 14, 2026 — approval secures funding for heritage programs and grants.

key_dates.label: Council vote on VHF annual report and grant

agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to choose sites for 2026-2029 public plaza drinking program
agenda_briefs.summary: Council approved 13 public plaza locations for the Alcohol Consumption in Public Plazas Program for a three-year cycle (2026-2028), plus a two-year extension for the Drinker's Lounge parklet at 111 Princess St. Permissible drinking hours align with nearby washroom operating hours. The program transitions from annual to a 3-year cycle; new plazas can join every 3 years.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Check if your business is near one of the 13 approved plaza sites — alcohol consumption hours will be set by nearby washroom schedules.

key_dates.label: Alcohol Consumption in Public Plazas Program 3-year cycle begins
Program cycle ends (next intake for new plazas)

agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.	Sites for 2026-2029 Alcohol Consumption in Public Plazas Program
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby seeks grant to speed up development approvals
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider endorsing a $200,000 grant application to the Union of BC Municipalities Local Government Development Approvals Program. The funding would be used to redesign approval processes for large-scale housing developments, including creating reusable guides, templates, and service blueprints. The application was submitted March 13, 2026. A decision on whether to apply is expected at the April 14 council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed now. Watch for council's decision — faster approvals could affect your project timelines.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.	Funding Application to Local Government Development Approvals Program
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on housing rezoning rule changes
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on amendments to housing rezoning conditions for 11 developments, including adjustments to below-market rental (BMR) affordability under the Rental Development Relief Program (RDRP). Eligible projects may adjust BMR rents from a 20% discount to CMHC city-wide average rents, reflecting up to a 25% discount vs. 2024 market rents. The RDRP is a two-year pilot (Feb 1, 2026 – Dec 15, 2027) for mid/high-rise BMR projects on sites under 8,000 sq m with fewer than 10 existing purpose-built rental units.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for council decision; if passed, affected property owners must agree to amended Housing Agreements.

key_dates.label: Rental Development Relief Program pilot period
Rental Development Relief Program pilot ends

agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Text amendment to CD-1 (839) bylaw for 5590 Victoria Drive and 2005 East 40th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on a text amendment to CD-1 (839) Bylaw No. 13493 for 5590 Victoria Drive and 2005 East 40th Avenue. The amendment would change the required housing from for-profit affordable rental (leased by a non-profit, with 30% of units for households below HILs, rent capped at 30% of income) to 100% secured market rental housing. The floor space ratio (FSR) would increase from 3.49 to 3.5 to allow larger units. No public hearing will be held because the proposal is consistent with the Official Development Plan and is majority residential.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date (April 14, 2026) and submit comments to Council before the meeting.

key_dates.label: Council meeting to vote on CD-1 text amendment — 5590 Victoria Drive

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: CD-1 Rezoning at 4088 Granville Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning bylaw for 4088 Granville Street. The draft bylaw would rezone the property from FSD to CD-1, increasing the maximum floor space ratio from 0.5 to 1.2 and building height from 11.5 m to 13.4 m, to permit three three-storey residential rental buildings with 31 units. At least 35% of units must have 2+ bedrooms. No public hearing is required as the proposal is consistent with the Official Development Plan.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — if you own property or a business nearby, you may want to attend or submit comments.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning for 3803-3823 West 10th Ave and 2553 Highbury St
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on a CD-1 rezoning for 3803-3823 West 10th Avenue and 2553 Highbury Street to allow a six-storey mixed-use building with 82 secured market rental units and commercial space (minimum 594.9 m²). The existing requirement for 20% below-market rental (BMR) floor area is deferred for two years: if a Stage 1 building permit is not issued within two years of Council's approval in principle, the BMR requirement will be reinstated. The maximum FSR is 3.50, building height is 22 m, and at least 35% of units must have 2+ bedrooms. The Sign By-law and Noise Control By-law will also be amended.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend the public hearing to have your say on this rezoning proposal.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: CD-1 Rezoning for 310 East 14th Avenue in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the property at 310 East 14th Avenue. This is a 'CD-1 Rezoning', which stands for Comprehensive Development Zone — a special zoning category that allows for a specific development plan, rather than following the standard rules for that area. The draft bylaw would amend the city's main Zoning and Development Bylaw. If approved, this would change what can be built on that property. The decision has not been made yet; council will consider the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — this rezoning could affect nearby property values or development plans.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning at 1305-1325 West 13th Avenue in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the properties at 1305-1325 West 13th Avenue from R3-3 to R5-4 District. If approved, the rezoning would allow a 20-storey residential building with 172 rental units, including 20% of residential floor area secured as below-market rental units. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend the public hearing to have your say on this rezoning proposal.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby amenity cost charges and density bonus updates
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider proposed updates to how the city charges developers for community amenities like parks, childcare, and community centres. A proposed bylaw would update the Amenity Cost Charge (ACC) — a fee developers pay to help fund new amenities needed because of growth. Council will also consider updates to the Density Bonus and Inclusionary Zoning policies, which let developers build more units in exchange for providing affordable housing or cash contributions. These changes are part of the 2026 Financing Growth Update. A public consultation is required before council can vote to approve the new bylaw and policy updates.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend or submit comments at the required public consultation to have your say on the proposed amenity cost charges and density bonus changes.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
16.	2026 Financing Growth Update – Required Consultation for Amenity Cost Charge By law and Bill 16 Density Bonus and Inclusionary Zoning Updates
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Bylaw to assess property owners for Trounce Alley & Blood Alley Square maintenance
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would charge property owners in the Trounce Alley and Blood Alley Square area for the 2025 maintenance costs of those public spaces. A 'local improvement project' is a way for a city to pay for upgrades or upkeep in a specific area by billing the properties that benefit from the work. If approved, this bylaw would add a special fee to the property taxes of affected landowners to cover things like cleaning, repairs, and landscaping for the alleys and square next year.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Check if your property is in the Trounce Alley or Blood Alley Square assessment area and watch for the council vote.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Food Service
agenda_briefs.source_body: 8.	A By-law to assess real property to defray 2025 maintenance costs for the Trounce Alley and Blood Alley Square Local Improvement Project
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw to assess parking costs on East Hastings
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would charge property owners along East Hastings Street to help pay for the 2025 costs of the East Hastings Street Collective Parking Project. A 'bylaw to assess real property' means the city would place a special fee on certain properties to cover the cost of a shared parking project. This is not yet approved — it is up for discussion and a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if your property is on East Hastings Street.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body: 7.	A By-law to assess real property to defray 2025 costs for the East Hastings Street Collective Parking Project
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Housing agreement bylaw for 320-360 West 2nd Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would create a housing agreement for the property at 320-360 West 2nd Avenue. A housing agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that locks in certain conditions, like how many affordable rental units must be provided and for how long. This proposal has not been approved yet — it is up for discussion and a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if you own or manage property near this site.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 6.	A By-law to enact a Housing Agreement for 320-360 West 2nd Avenue

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Amendment to housing agreement for Prior Street, Malkin Avenue, Station Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would amend an existing Housing Agreement for properties at 450-496 Prior Street, 550 Malkin Avenue, and 1002 Station Street. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that sets rules for how the property is used, often to keep it affordable or reserved for certain types of housing. This amendment would change the terms of that agreement. The decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 5.	A By-law to authorize the amendment of a Housing Agreement Authorized by By-law No. 14507 (450 – 496 Prior Street, 550 Malkin Avenue and 1002 Station Street)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on zoning amendment at 1700-1750 West 75th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the zoning rules for the property at 1700-1750 West 75th Avenue. This is an amendment to an existing Comprehensive Development (CD-1) bylaw, which is a special zoning plan for a specific site. The change could affect what can be built or how the land can be used at that address. The decision has not been made yet — council will discuss and vote on it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if you own or operate a business at this location.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning at 1710-1730 East Pender Street to CD-1
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the properties at 1710-1730 East Pender Street. The change would create a new 'CD-1' zone, which stands for Comprehensive Development Zone. This is a special zoning category that allows for a mix of uses or a specific development plan, rather than following the standard rules for that area. The decision has not been made yet — this is an upcoming item on the council agenda.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you own property or a business near 1710-1730 East Pender Street, watch for the council decision — this rezoning could affect future development or property values in your area.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning at 6486 Chester Street to CD-1
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the property at 6486 Chester Street to a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) zone. A CD-1 zone is a custom zoning category that allows for specific types of development not normally permitted under standard zoning rules. This is a proposed change only — it has not been approved yet. Council will consider the bylaw at an upcoming meeting.

agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the properties at 1302–1318 East 12th Avenue to a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) zone. A CD-1 zone is a custom zoning category that allows for a specific development plan, rather than following the standard rules for that area. This is a first step — the bylaw has not been approved yet, and Council will debate and decide on it at a future meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning for 888-896 Cambie Street and 111-191 Smithe Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning for properties at 888-896 Cambie Street and 111-191 Smithe Street. This is a 'CD-1 Rezoning', which means the land would be reclassified as a Comprehensive Development Zone. If approved, this would allow a specific development plan for the site, changing what can be built there. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider a referral report at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments to Burnaby City Council before the vote to share your views on this proposed rezoning.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: CD-1 rezoning for 888 West 8th Avenue in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning for 888 West 8th Avenue. This would change the land use rules for this property to a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) zone, increasing the maximum floor space ratio from 3.0 to 8.1 and the maximum building height from 9.2 m (30 ft.) to 50.6 m (166 ft.). The proposal is at the referral stage, meaning council is considering whether to approve it without a public hearing because it is consistent with the Vancouver Official Development Plan and the Broadway Plan. If approved, this would allow a 16-storey commercial building containing 152 hotel rooms and ground-floor commercial space.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council meeting date — this is your chance to comment on the proposed rezoning if Council decides to hold a public hearing.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
2.	CD-1 Rezoning: 888 West 8th Avenue
Referral Report

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Fast-track rental housing and small hotels in new Burnaby commercial district
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has directed staff to consult on a proposed Amenity Cost Charge (ACC) By-law and updates to density bonus and inclusionary zoning rules required by provincial Bill 16. The ACC would fund amenities like community centres and libraries. The Bill 16 updates must be adopted by June 30, 2026. A workshop for development industry stakeholders is planned for May 2026, and the ACC By-law is expected for Council consideration on July 28, 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Development industry stakeholders should watch for consultation notices and consider participating in the May 2026 workshop on the proposed Amenity Cost Charge By-law.

key_dates.label: Bill 16 density bonus rules compliance deadline
ACC By-law expected for Council consideration
